aboveYou.name: Plum Heating & Cooling
aboveYou.note: Also outranks you for 6 related searches

aboveYou.name: Specair
aboveYou.note: Running Google Ads on top of their organic spot

findings.text: Your site takes 7.2 seconds to appear on a phone
findings.detail: Most emergency heating searches happen on mobile. Under 2.5 seconds is the target; past 4, half your visitors give up.

findings.text: You do not appear in the map pack for your own suburb
findings.detail: The three map results above the regular listings take the majority of calls for urgent repair searches.

findings.text: Your homepage title does not mention Werribee or repairs
findings.detail: It currently reads like a brand name only. The title is the strongest signal Google has for matching you to a search.

findings.text: 11 of your last 20 Google reviews have no reply
findings.detail: Unanswered reviews read as an unattended business, to Google and to the customer choosing between you and the next result.

findings.text: Your site is secure and mobile-friendly
findings.detail: The foundations are fine. The problem is visibility, not the website itself.

stealPoint.quote: Booked a heater service two weeks ago, nobody showed and nobody called back. Had to chase them twice to get a refund.
stealPoint.insight: The businesses above you are winning on visibility, not on service. Their own customers say response time is the weak point. A same-day-callback promise, made visible where people compare you, takes that advantage apart.
